subject: ap-precalculus
title: Logarithmic Regression: Soil Temperature & Depth
stimulus:
A geologist measures soil temperature (in degrees Celsius) at various depths below the surface and records the following data:

| Depth $d$ (meters) | Temperature $T$ ($^\circ$C) |
|---|---|
| 1 | 12.0 |
| 4 | 18.4 |
| 9 | 22.6 |
| 16 | 26.0 |
| 25 | 28.7 |

The geologist proposes the model $T(d) = a + b\ln(d)$ to represent soil temperature as a function of depth.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the data at $d = 1$ and $d = 25$ to write two equations that can be used to find the values of constants $a$ and $b$.

(ii) Find the values of $a$ and $b$ as decimal approximations. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: First equation: $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0 \Rightarrow a = 12.0$ (since $\ln(1) = 0$). Second equation: $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$. Solving: $b = 16.7/\ln(25) \approx 16.7/3.2189 \approx 5.189$ (accept $[5.18, 5.20]$). Decimal accuracy: 3 places. Full credit for $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ as a complete response.

scoring_points.description: Two correct equations: $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$ (giving $a = 12$) and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$
common_traps: $\ln(1) = 0$, so the first equation immediately gives $a = 12.0$ — this simplification is key

scoring_points.description: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b \approx 5.189$; supporting work must show the division step
common_traps: Decimal presentation: $b$ must be given to at least 3 decimal places — $5.189$, not $5.19$

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to predict the soil temperature at a depth of $d = 50$ meters. Express your answer as a decimal approximation.

(ii) Find the depth $d$ at which the model predicts a soil temperature of $30^\circ$C. Express your answer as a decimal approximation. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $T(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot \ln(50) \approx 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 \approx 12 + 20.30 \approx 32.30$°C (accept $[32.2, 32.4]$). For part (ii), $5.189\ln(d) = 18 \Rightarrow \ln(d) = 18/5.189 \approx 3.469 \Rightarrow d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ m (accept $[31.5, 32.7]$). Supporting work must show both the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion.

scoring_points.description: Correct prediction $T(50) \approx 32.30$°C (accept $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution of $d = 50$
common_traps: The model uses $\ln(d)$, not $d$ — substituting $50$ directly without taking $\ln$ gives a completely wrong answer

scoring_points.description: Correct depth $d \approx 32.1$ meters (accept $[31.5, 32.7]$); supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting to exponential form
common_traps: Two steps required: (1) isolate $\ln(d)$ by dividing by $b$, (2) convert to exponential form $d = e^{\text{result}}$

parts.prompt:
(i) The geologist considers a linear model $T_L(d) = 12 + 0.68d$ fitted through $d = 1$ and $d = 25$. Find the value of $T_L(9)$ and compare it to the actual data value $T(9) = 22.6$°C. Determine whether the linear model overestimates or underestimates at $d = 9$.

(ii) Use the logarithmic model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to solve the inequality $T(d) > 25$. Determine the range of depths $d$ for which the model predicts the soil temperature exceeds $25^\circ$C. Express your answer as a decimal approximation with supporting work.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $T_L(9) = 12 + 0.68(9) = 12 + 6.12 = 18.12$°C vs. actual $22.6$°C — the linear model underestimates. For part (ii), $5.189\ln(d) > 13 \Rightarrow \ln(d) > 13/5.189 \approx 2.506 \Rightarrow d > e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ m (accept $[12.1, 12.4]$). Supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting.

scoring_points.description: $T_L(9) = 18.12$°C; the linear model underestimates the actual temperature of $22.6$°C at $d = 9$

alternatives.correct_example: $T_L(9) = 12 + 0.68(9) = 12 + 6.12 = 18.12$°C. Since $18.12 < 22.6$, the linear model underestimates the actual temperature at $d = 9$ meters.

wrong_examples: 'Overestimates' — incorrect; $18.12 < 22.6$, so the linear model gives a lower temperature than actual
Not comparing to the actual data value $22.6$°C
common_traps: Underestimate means the model predicts LESS than reality; $T_L(9) = 18.12 < 22.6 = T(9)$, so the linear model underestimates

scoring_points.description: Correct depth range $d > 12.26$ meters (accept $[12.1, 12.4]$); supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting to exponential form
wrong_examples: Giving a specific value instead of an interval ($d > 12.26$, not $d = 12.26$)
common_traps: The answer is an inequality $d > 12.26$ because we are solving $T(d) > 25$, not $T(d) = 25$ — express as a range
